The Trump administration’s decision to give Live Nation a light slap on the wrist is a betrayal of every music lover and concertgoer exploited by Live Nation-Ticketmaster.

posts.text:
The unemployment rate rose to 4.4% in February. Young workers and workers of color are being hit hardest.

Nearly 2 million Americans have been out of work for over six months.

Meanwhile, Trump is pouring millions of dollars into renovating his gold-encrusted ballroom.

posts.text:
Instead of helping Americans drowning in student loan debt, Trump is making life worse for borrowers—including potentially selling the federal student loan portfolio.

If the Trump administration cared about working-class families, it wouldn’t sell them out to Wall Street.

posts.text:
Trump has stolen over $1,700 from each American family through his illegal tariffs.

Not returning the money would be theft in broad daylight from each and every American family that has paid the price of Trump’s chaotic tariffs.

posts.text:
What is Trump’s justification for attacking Iran?

What’s his plan to get out?

What constitutes “winning” here?

Is this another Forever War?

The American people deserve real answers, not contradictions or lies.

Meanwhile, the Republicans in Congress claim we can’t afford to fund education, child care, housing, or health care.

posts.text:
BREAKING NEWS: Donald "Art of the Deal" Trump settled the Ticketmaster-Live Nation antitrust case.

If you love going to concerts, Trump's deal means you'll keep paying a "Ticketmaster Tax."

And artists will keep getting bullied.

It's time to break up Ticketmaster-Live Nation.

posts.text:
Capping credit card interest rates would save families $100 billion a year.

Keeping rates high helps pay the salaries and bonuses of credit card company executives.

By not capping credit card rates, Trump is choosing corporate CEOs over the American people.
